meta.title: Pediatric Practice Software | Well-Child Efficiency & Same-Day Sick | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to pediatric practice operations. Well-child visit optimization. Same-day sick access. Vaccine administration tracking. Behavioral health screening.

hero.subhead: Your well-child visits take too long. Your sick visits can't get same-day access. Your vaccine tracking is fragmented. clinIQ optimizes well-child efficiency, enables true same-day sick access, and streamlines vaccine administration.

sections.content:
Pediatric practices face unique operational challenges: high visit volumes, seasonal variability, age-specific visit requirements, and parental expectations.

**The well-child bottleneck:**

Well-child visits include developmental assessment, anticipatory guidance, screening questionnaires, and vaccinations. All of this takes time. Most practices schedule 20-30 minutes but actually need 30-40 minutes. Visits run behind.

**The sick visit access problem:**

Parents expect same-day sick access. 'My child is sick' should result in a same-day appointment. But well-child visits fill the schedule. Sick slots are gone by 10 AM.

**The vaccine coordination burden:**

Vaccine schedules are complex. Different vaccines at different ages with different combinations. Documentation for state registries. Inventory management. Lot number tracking.

**The seasonal swing:**

Pediatrics has extreme seasonality. Summer is slow (well-childs). Winter is slammed (respiratory illness). Staffing and scheduling must adapt.

Well-child visits are the core of pediatric revenue but often run inefficiently.

**What slows well-child visits:**

Screening questionnaires (ASQ, M-CHAT, PHQ-A, etc.) done in room instead of before.

Anticipatory guidance delivered in conversation instead of pre-visit materials.

Vaccine consent obtained during visit instead of before.

Visit scheduled for 20 minutes when it needs 30.

**What clinIQ does:**

Pre-visit questionnaires: Age-appropriate screening sent before visit. ASQ, M-CHAT, PHQ-A — completed by parent before arrival.

Anticipatory guidance: Age-specific guidance sent before visit. Parent arrives prepared.

Vaccine pre-consent: Vaccine consent reviewed and signed before visit.

Age-specific scheduling: Different ages have different visit lengths. 2-month well-child differs from 15-year annual.

**The throughput improvement:**

10 minutes saved per well-child × 10 well-childs/day = 100 minutes = 2-3 additional visits/day.

At $150/well-child, that's $300-450/day = $75,000-115,000/year.

Parents expect same-day access for sick children. Meeting this expectation builds loyalty and prevents urgent care/ED leakage.

**Why same-day sick fails:**

Fixed sick slots are gone early. Schedule is full of well-childs. No visibility into where capacity exists.

**What clinIQ does:**

Real-time capacity visibility: Where can sick visits fit? Which providers are running ahead? Where are gaps?

Well-child efficiency: Faster well-childs create capacity for sick visits.

Dynamic scheduling: Instead of fixed sick slots, identify capacity throughout the day.

Nurse triage integration: Triage call determines urgency. Schedule accordingly.

**The parent experience:**

Parent calls at 10 AM: 'My child has a fever.'

Old response: 'We don't have any openings today. Can you come tomorrow or go to urgent care?'

New response: 'We can see you at 2:30 PM today.'

That difference determines whether families stay with your practice.

Pediatric vaccine schedules are complex. Different vaccines at different ages with different combinations and catch-up protocols.

**What clinIQ does:**

Vaccine due: Age-calculated vaccine recommendations. What's due at this visit?

Vaccine history: Complete vaccination record. What's been given? What's missing?

Lot tracking: Lot numbers documented. Required for registry reporting.

Registry integration: State immunization registry connection where available.

VFC inventory: If you participate in VFC, separate tracking for VFC vs private stock.

**Catch-up scheduling:**

Child comes in behind on vaccines. What's the optimal catch-up schedule? Which vaccines can be given together? What needs to wait?

Pediatric behavioral health screening is increasingly important:

- M-CHAT for autism (18-24 months)
- ASQ for developmental delay
- PHQ-A for adolescent depression
- SCARED for anxiety
- Vanderbilt for ADHD

**What clinIQ does:**

Age-appropriate screening: Right screening at right age.

Pre-visit administration: Parents complete screening before arrival.

Scoring: Automatic scoring with flag for positive screens.

Referral tracking: Positive screen → referral → referral completed.

**Week 1: Configuration**

Well-child optimization, screening questionnaires, vaccine tracking configured.

**Week 2: Training and Go-Live**

Staff learns pre-visit workflow. Providers learn same-day visibility.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Pre-visit questionnaires reducing visit time. Same-day visibility improving.

Month 3: Well-child throughput measurably improved. Same-day sick access achieved.

Month 6: Full optimization. Additional visits generating revenue.

**ROI Math:**

Well-child efficiency: 2 additional visits/day × $150 × 250 days = $75,000/year

Patient retention: Families who get same-day sick access stay with practice

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Can RTM be billed for pediatric patients?
faqs.a: Yes, for appropriate conditions. Asthma, diabetes, obesity, behavioral health conditions can all qualify for RTM in pediatric patients. Parent/guardian involvement is key.
Age-appropriate questionnaires sent to parents before visit. They complete on phone or computer. Results available when child arrives.

faqs.q: Does this integrate with state registries?
faqs.a: We support state immunization registry integration where electronic interfaces are available.

faqs.q: What about sports physicals and school forms?
faqs.a: These visit types can be configured with appropriate duration and pre-visit requirements.

testimonial.quote: Pre-visit questionnaires changed everything. Parents arrive prepared. Visits actually fit in the schedule.
